ui.tagline: How to sort your waste at home
ui.intro: Sort your waste into the right bin or bag, and keep the types apart. Then as much as possible can be reused.
ui.legalNote: This content is based on the national standard sortere.no.
ui.scanHint: Scan for the online version in more languages
ui.quizIntro: Where should the item go? Tap the right bin.
ui.quizBelongPrompt: Does this go in this bin?

om.title: About the centre
om.lead: Vensmoen reception centre is located at Røkland in Saltdal municipality, Nordland.

regler.title: House rules
regler.lead: The rules apply to everyone living at the centre and are available here in several languages.

guide.title: Practical guide
guide.lead: The essentials of everyday life in Norway, explained briefly.

matavfall.name: Food waste
matavfall.bin: Green food bin. Always use a biodegradable bag and tie it. The bags are free in the shop and at the recycling station.
accepted: Kitchen paper with food on it
matavfall.note: No bio-bag? Wrap wet food waste in newspaper or a paper bag.

papir.name: Cardboard and paper
papir.bin: Blue paper bin. Put it in loose – NOT in a bag. Fold or cut up big boxes.
papir.note: It must be clean and dry. Rinse and flatten milk cartons.

plast.name: Plastic packaging
plast.bin: A separate transparent bag – tie it shut. The bag is free in the shop and at the recycling station.
rejected: Plastic with lots of food on it that will not wash off
plast.note: Rinse the packaging and let it dry. If it stays dirty, put it in residual waste.

glassmetall.name: Glass and metal packaging
glassmetall.bin: Shared bin for glass and metal. Put it in loose – NOT in a bag. Rinse it first.
accepted: Glass bottles from medicine, beer and wine
Jam jars and other food jars
Beer and soda cans without Norwegian deposit
rejected: Window glass, light bulbs, porcelain and mirrors
glassmetall.note: Only packaging (what the food or drink came in). Pots and pans go to the recycling station.

restavfall.name: Residual waste
restavfall.bin: The residual waste bin at home. Pack it in a bag and tie it well.
accepted: Bag of dog waste and cat litter
rejected: Food, paper, plastic, glass and metal – these must be sorted
restavfall.note: Residual waste is what is LEFT once you have sorted out the rest. Sort as much as you can first – it costs less.

farlig.name: Hazardous waste and electronics
farlig.bin: NEVER in any bin at home. Take batteries, light bulbs and small electronics to the shop that sells them, or to the recycling station.
accepted: Phones, chargers, cables and small electronics
Paint, glue, varnish and spray cans
rejected: Never in the toilet, sink or drain
farlig.note: Batteries in residual waste can catch fire in the rubbish truck.
